personality_belief.metaphysical_profile: Used only as cultural flavor, never as a decision rule.
personality_belief.decision_style: practical and constrained by real workflow trade-offs
personality_belief.trust_orientation: needs proof from concrete workflows, not just positioning
personality_belief.self_image: responsible, busy, and careful about adding complexity

economic_profile.purchase_decision_process: tests fit against current workflow before committing

life_goals: stay on top of work without constant catch-up
protect personal bandwidth for family and recovery
fears: paying for another tool that becomes shelfware
making work messier or losing client trust
aspirations: reduce admin drag to focus on client delivery and growth
look more organised and trustworthy to partners
moral_boundaries: will not trade away other people's privacy for convenience
expects transparent pricing with no hidden renewal traps
status_concerns: does not want to appear disorganised in front of clients or team
prefers steady competence over flashy innovation
values.trade_offs: Willing to sacrifice minor convenience features if they threaten workflow clarity or require excessive onboarding time. Prioritises predictable monthly costs over free tiers with data limits.

life_story.childhood_background: Grew up in a household that valued practical decisions and financial caution in a regional sea service ops context.
life_story.education_journey: Completed diploma level education and learned through a mix of formal training and on-the-job repetition during a early adult small business builder phase.
life_story.career_path: Transitioned into small business ownership after recognising that reliability and aligned operations reward consistency more than speed. Currently operates with repeatable workflows but faces scaling friction as client volume grows.
life_story.family_story: Lives in a four-person multi-generational household in Kuching. High caregiving load means administrative windows are fragmented. Family expectations reinforce a cautious approach to new commitments and financial risk.
life_story.current_daily_routine: Weekdays split between client coordination, message follow-ups, and protecting narrow blocks of focused work. Balances business calls with family check-ins. Uses medium schedule pressure to triage tasks, relying on deliberate decision speed to avoid reactive mistakes.
recent_life_events: recently audited software subscriptions and cut two underused tools
attempting to reduce context-switching by batching communications
negotiated a stable contract renewal that lowered immediate cash-flow anxiety
frustrations: client context scattered across WhatsApp, email, and notes
family responsibilities making admin feel heavier and less forgiving
hidden_needs: wants confidence that a new tool will simplify the week within three days
needs a low-stakes way to test workflow fit before committing real data
seeks reassurance that onboarding won't disrupt existing client commitments

behavior_profile.buying_behavior: Waits until value is concrete enough to justify habit change. Prefers phased adoption: start with one workflow, validate results, then expand.
information_sources: peer referrals via WhatsApp business groups
short product demos or sandbox trials
comparison notes against current manual processes
social_media_usage: LinkedIn for industry benchmarks and vendor credibility
WhatsApp groups for practical tips and local vendor recommendations
behavior_profile.referral_influence: Moderate to high. Trusts recommendations from operators in similar regional markets who face comparable constraints.
brand_trust_signals: specific user stories showing before/after time savings
visible privacy and data handling stance
decision_blockers: uncertain ROI or vague time-saving claims
messy setup requiring data migration or staff training
trust gap around data residency or sharing practices
emotional_triggers: feeling more in control of follow-ups and deadlines
confidence that the tool respects existing boundaries
behavior_profile.workflow_maturity: Repeatable but brittle under unexpected volume spikes.
behavior_profile.documentation_discipline: Medium. Keeps structured notes but struggles with consistency when caregiving or client emergencies interrupt.
behavior_profile.manager_approval_dependence: Low. As owner-decider, retains full purchase authority but self-regulates against unnecessary spend.

active_pain_points: fragmented communication across messaging apps and email
manual follow-up creating evening catch-up cycles
lack of a single source of truth for client next-actions
caregiving load compressing available admin time
latent_pain_points: mental load from tracking loose ends across platforms
slow drift between promises made and actual follow-through
quiet stress from fearing missed deadlines or client disappointment
jobs_to_be_done: capture next actions reliably without double-entry
avoid dropped tasks during high-pressure periods
maintain professional presentation without extra overhead
current_workaround: manual checklists in notebook or basic apps
problem_context.urgency_level: Medium. Not critical failure, but accumulating friction reduces quality of life and margin for error.
problem_context.willingness_to_change: Medium. Open to shifts if they demonstrably save time without disrupting client relationships.
problem_context.willingness_to_pay: Medium. Comfortable with upper-middle budget range if ROI is visible within two weeks.
problem_context.proof_threshold: Visible trial. Needs to run a realistic scenario with sample data before committing.

sensitive_reality_layer.social_risk_profile: Highly conscious of appearing careless with colleague or client information. Avoids sharing unverified claims or tools that lack clear governance.
sensitive_reality_layer.fairness_and_inclusion_profile: Expects product language to remain respectful, culturally aware, and not assume a one-size-fits-all workflow. Prefers neutral, professional framing.
sensitive_reality_layer.taboo_topic_comfort: Low. Disengages from polarising or overly personal product messaging.
sensitive_reality_layer.political_sensitivity: Low. Focuses strictly on operational utility and commercial terms.
sensitive_reality_layer.discrimination_awareness: Medium. Notices when onboarding or support assumes certain tech privileges or ignores accessibility constraints.
sensitive_reality_layer.public_expression_risk_aversion: Medium. Prefers private evaluation and controlled feedback channels over public beta testing.
sensitive_reality_layer.identity_labeling_comfort: Low. Resists being categorised as a demographic stereotype; responds better to role-based validation.
response_boundaries: will not share private third-party data without clear consent and purpose
declines marketing that overpromises automation without human oversight
expects straightforward privacy explanations in plain language
sensitive_reality_layer.public_explanation_preference: Plain language with concrete examples, regional relevance, and explicit data handling commitments.

audit_evidence_layer.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.
